title: quassia cuneata
intro: Quassia cuneata, also known as the yellow quassia or South American quassia, is a plant species native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. It is a small shrub or tree with medicinal properties, particularly in its roots, which have been used for centuries in traditional medicine to treat a variety of health issues. The roots of Quassia cuneata contain quassin, a compound with antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antiparasitic properties, which have been shown to provide health benefits such as reducing fever, relieving pain, and fighting off infections. Quassia cuneata can be prepared as a tincture, infusion, or decoction, and it is also used in herbal remedies to treat conditions like malaria, fever, and skin diseases.
